name: Crab Casserole in a Clay Pot
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Fresh whole crab, preferably live
2 Unpeeled fresh ginger slices, 1/4 inch thick
2 Fresh red chili peppers, seeded and shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. If using a live crab, prepare it according to the technique on page 77 (rinse, scrub, and steam for ten minutes).
2. Cut the body into quarters and lightly crack the claws and legs.
3. Heat a wok or large skillet until hot, then add the peanut oil.
4. Add the garlic, ginger, and scallions. Stir-fry to flavor the oil.
5. Add the black beans, chili peppers, and crab. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
6. Pour in the rice wine, light soy sauce, and chicken stock.
7. Transfer the contents to a clay pot or casserole dish and cover.
8. Cook over high heat for 5 more minutes, or until the crab shell turns bright red.
9. Serve immediately.
Makes 4 servings.
